hints.Deus: 'Deus' is a noun meaning 'God' — it is the subject
hints.mortuorum: 'Mortuorum' is a substantive adjective/participle in the genitive plural
hints.vivorum: 'Vivorum' is a substantive adjective/participle in the genitive plural

hints.to eat: 'To eat' is 'edere' (infinitive also 'esse') — here 'est' is from 'sum' (to be)
hints.to go out: 'To go out' is 'exire' — 'est' is from 'sum, esse'

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sit' — 'est' is indicative

hints.edo, esse, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edo' (to eat) — same infinitive but different verb
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the parts of 'sto' (to stand)
hints.exsisto, exsistere, exstiti, —: These are the parts of 'exsisto' (to exist/arise)

hints.vocative: Vocative of 'Deus' is 'Deus' (or 'Dee'), but here it is the subject of 'est'
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'Deum' — 'Deus' is the nominative subject
hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'Dei' — 'Deus' is the nominative subject

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'Dei' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'Dei' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -us — 'Dei' ends in -i

hints.accusative plural: Accusative plural would be 'mortuos' — 'mortuorum' with -orum is genitive
hints.dative plural: Dative plural would be 'mortuis' — 'mortuorum' with -orum is genitive
hints.ablative plural: Ablative plural would be 'mortuis' — 'mortuorum' with -orum is genitive

hints.3rd declension adjective: 3rd declension adjectives have forms like -is, -e — 'mortuus, -a, -um' is 1st/2nd
hints.4th declension: Adjectives do not follow the 4th declension

hints.nominative plural: Nominative plural would be 'vivi' — 'vivorum' with -orum is genitive
hints.accusative plural: Accusative plural would be 'vivos' — 'vivorum' with -orum is genitive
hints.dative plural: Dative plural would be 'vivis' — 'vivorum' with -orum is genitive

steps.prompt: What is the grammatical use of 'mortuorum' and 'vivorum' here?

hints.attributive adjectives modifying 'Deus': They are in the genitive, not nominative — they don't modify 'Deus' directly
hints.predicate adjectives: Predicate adjectives agree with the subject — these are genitive, not nominative
hints.ablative absolutes: Ablative absolutes require the ablative case — these are genitive

steps.prompt: What type of genitive is 'mortuorum' / 'vivorum' here?
steps.reveal: He is [the God] of the living = He belongs to / is in relationship with the living

hints.partitive genitive: Partitive genitive means 'part of' — this describes God's relationship to the living
hints.objective genitive: Objective genitive shows the object of an implied action — this is about relationship/possession
hints.genitive of description: Genitive of description qualifies a noun's character — this is about relationship

translation: God is not the God of the dead but of the living
